response.justification: The candidate brings nearly two decades of broad engineering experience, genuine high-throughput systems work (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), strong TypeScript, and recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling experience at re:cinq — a direct match for the AI-native mindset requirement. However, Python is a must-have and appears nowhere on the resume, the Staff-level tenure is short (six months), and most of the career reflects senior IC delivery rather than directing technical strategy and cross-functional stakeholder coordination at scale. Worth a conversation if Python depth can be verified, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second)
Recent AI-native experience: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling
Strong TypeScript/Node.js plus craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No Python listed anywhere despite it being a must-have skill
Limited evidence of Staff-scope technical strategy and stakeholder leadership; Staff title held only ~6 months
Current career gap and most intense distributed-systems work dates to 2018–2020

key_factors.factor: AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling experience
